name: Queue Basics
description: A queue follows FIFO order: the earliest inserted item leaves first.
exampleJava: Queue<Integer> q = new ArrayDeque<>(); q.offer(10); int front = q.peek();
commonMistakes: Treating a queue like a stack
Removing from the wrong end

name: Enqueue Dequeue
description: Core queue operations should insert at the rear and remove from the front in O(1).
commonMistakes: Using remove(0) on an ArrayList
Forgetting empty checks before poll or peek

name: Array-Based Queue
description: Implement a queue with an array and front/rear pointers.
commonMistakes: Not handling capacity limits
Letting front and rear grow without control

name: Circular Queue
description: Wrap front and rear indices with modulo arithmetic so space can be reused.
commonMistakes: Confusing full and empty states
Applying modulo in only one pointer update

name: Queue Simulation
description: Simulate FIFO flows directly instead of reasoning abstractly.
exampleJava: for (int x : values) { q.offer(x); if (needRemove) q.poll(); }
commonMistakes: Skipping intermediate queue states
Removing multiple elements without checking availability

name: Generate Binary Numbers
description: Use a queue of strings to generate values level by level.
commonMistakes: Appending to the wrong parent string
Using recursion instead of queue layering

name: Breadth-First Queue Flow
description: Use a queue to process states layer by layer in shortest-step style problems.
commonMistakes: Revisiting states repeatedly
Mixing DFS recursion with BFS intent

name: Sliding Window with Deque
description: Use a deque to keep only useful candidates for the current window.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to remove out-of-window indices
Storing values instead of indices when positions matter

name: Deque Technique
description: Use both ends of a deque deliberately for front expiry and back cleanup.
commonMistakes: Removing from the wrong end
Using a plain queue where a deque is required

name: Top-K with Priority Queue
description: Use a heap to keep only the strongest K candidates efficiently.
commonMistakes: Using a full sort when K is much smaller than n
Choosing the wrong heap direction

name: Task Scheduling Queue
description: Model scheduling and cooldown constraints with queues or heaps.
commonMistakes: Not releasing cooled tasks back in time
Using only one structure when two phases are needed
